paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Henry Moore was born in Castleford, a small town near Leeds in the north of England. He was the seventh child of Raymond Moore and his wife Mary Baker. He studied at Castleford Grammar School from 1909 to 1915, where his early interest in art was encouraged by his teacher Alice Gostick. After leaving school, Moore hoped to become a sculptor, but instead he complied with his father's wish that he train as a schoolteacher. He had to abandon his training in 1917 when he was sent to France to fight in the First World War.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: After the war, Moore enrolled at the Leeds School of Art, where he studied for two years. In his first year, he spent most of his time drawing. Although he wanted to study sculpture, no teacher was appointed until his second year. At the end of that year, he passed the sculpture examination and was awarded a scholarship to the Royal College of Art in London. In September 1921, he moved to London and began three years of advanced study in sculpture.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Alongside the instruction he received at the Royal College, Moore visited many of the London museums, particularly the British Museum, which had a wide-ranging collection of ancient sculpture. During these visits, he discovered the power and beauty of ancient Egyptian and African sculpture. As he became increasingly interested in these 'primitive' forms of art, he turned away from European sculptural traditions.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: After graduating, Moore spent the first six months of 1925 travelling in France. When he visited the Trocadero Museum in Paris, he was impressed by a cast of a Mayan* sculpture of the rain spirit. It was a male reclining figure with its knees drawn up together, and its head at a right angle to its body. Moore became fascinated with this stone sculpture, which he thought had a power and originality that no other stone sculpture possessed. He himself started carving a variety of subjects in stone, including depictions of reclining women, mother-and-child groups, and masks.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Moore's exceptional talent soon gained recognition, and in 1926 he started work as a sculpture instructor at the Royal College. In 1933, he became a member of a group of young artists called Unit One. The aim of the group was to convince the English public of the merits of the emerging international movement in modern art and architecture.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Around this time, Moore moved away from the human figure to experiment with abstract shapes. In 1931, he held an exhibition at the Leicester Galleries in London. His work was enthusiastically welcomed by fellow sculptors, but the reviews in the press were extremely negative and turned Moore into a notorious figure. There were calls for his resignation from the Royal College, and the following year, when his contract expired, he left to start a sculpture department at the Chelsea School of Art in London.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Throughout the 1930s, Moore did not show any inclination to please the British public. He became interested in the paintings of the Spanish artist Pablo Picasso, whose work inspired him to distort the human body in a radical way. At times, he seemed to abandon the human figure altogether. The pages of his sketchbooks from this period show his ideas for abstract sculptures that bore little resemblance to the human form.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: In 1940, during the Second World War, Moore stopped teaching at the Chelsea School and moved to a farmhouse about 20 miles north of London. A shortage of materials forced him to focus on drawing. He did numerous small sketches of Londoners, later turning these ideas into large coloured drawings in his studio. In 1942, he returned to Castleford to make a series of sketches of the miners who worked there.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: In 1944, Harlow, a town near London, offered Moore a commission for a sculpture depicting a family. The resulting work signifies a dramatic change in Moore's style, away from the experimentation of the 1930s towards a more natural and humanistic subject matter. He did dozens of studies in clay for the sculpture, and these were cast in bronze and issued in editions of seven to nine copies each. In this way, Moore's work became available to collectors all over the world. The boost to his income enabled him to take on ambitious projects and start working on the scale he felt his sculpture demanded.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Critics who had begun to think that Moore had become less revolutionary were proven wrong by the appearance, in 1950, of the first of Moore's series of standing figures in bronze, with their harsh and angular pierced forms and distinct impression of menace. Moore also varied his subject matter in the 1950s with such works as Warrior with Shield and Falling Warrior. These were rare examples of Moore's use of the male figure and owe something to his visit to Greece in 1951, when he had the opportunity to study ancient works of art.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: In his final years, Moore created the Henry Moore Foundation to promote art appreciation and to display his work. Moore was the first modern English sculptor to achieve international critical acclaim and he is still regarded as one of the most important sculptors of the 20th century.

groups.desc:
Questions 1–7

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1–7 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information 
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information 
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: On leaving school, Moore did what his father wanted him to do.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“leaving school”和“did what his father wanted”在原文中分别对应“leaving school”和“complied with his father's wish”。原文明确提到摩尔离开学校后，虽然他希望成为雕塑家，但他遵从了父亲的愿望，接受了教师培训。这与题干描述一致，因此答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: After leaving school, Moore hoped to become a sculptor, but instead he complied with his father's wish that he train as a schoolteacher.
central_sentences.translation: 离开学校后，摩尔希望成为一名雕塑家，但他最终还是遵从了父亲的意愿，去接受了教师培训。

target_sen.target_sen: After leaving school, Moore hoped to become a sculptor, but instead he complied with his father's wish that he train as a schoolteacher.
After leaving school, Moore hoped to become a sculptor, but instead he complied with his father's wish that he train as a schoolteacher.

questions.title: Moore began studying sculpture in his first term at the Leeds School of Art.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Moore在第一学期开始学习雕塑”与文章内容相矛盾。文章明确提到Moore在第一年主要学习画画，并且没有雕塑老师，直到第二年才开始学习雕塑。因此，正确答案是FALSE。这道题的考点在于识别和理解时间及事件的关系，即使词汇量有限，通过识别关键信息和上下文也能做对。

central_sentences.sentence: In his first year, he spent most of his time drawing. Although he wanted to study sculpture, no teacher was appointed until his second year.
central_sentences.translation: 在他的第一年，他大部分时间都在画画。尽管他想学习雕塑，但直到第二年才任命了雕塑老师。

target_sen.target_sen: In his first year, he spent most of his time drawing.
Although he wanted to study sculpture, no teacher was appointed until his second year.
After the war, Moore enrolled at the Leeds School of Art, where he studied for two years.

questions.title: When Moore started at the Royal College of Art, its reputation for teaching sculpture was excellent.
questions.analysis: 题干中的关键信息是 "When Moore started at the Royal College of Art" 和 "its reputation for teaching sculpture was excellent"。我们在文章中找到了与 "Royal College of Art" 和 "teaching sculpture" 对应的表达，但没有找到关于学院声誉的信息。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到对应的表达，正确答案是 "NOT GIVEN"。

central_sentences.sentence: In September 1921, he moved to London and began three years of advanced study in sculpture.
central_sentences.translation: 1921年9月，他搬到伦敦，开始了三年的高级雕塑学习。

target_sen.target_sen: At the end of that year, he passed the sculpture examination and was awarded a scholarship to the Royal College of Art in London.

questions.title: Moore became aware of ancient sculpture as a result of visiting London museums.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "Moore became aware of ancient sculpture as a result of visiting London museums" 在文章中找到了直接对应的句子。题干中的 "became aware of" 对应文章中的 "discovered"，"ancient sculpture" 对应 "ancient Egyptian and African sculpture"，"visiting London museums" 对应 "visited many of the London museums"。这些对应关系表明题干信息在文章中得到了明确的支持，因此答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Alongside the instruction he received at the Royal College, Moore visited many of the London museums, particularly the British Museum, which had a wide-ranging collection of ancient sculpture. During these visits, he discovered the power and beauty of ancient Egyptian and African sculpture.
central_sentences.translation: 除了在皇家学院接受的教育外，Moore还参观了许多伦敦的博物馆，特别是拥有广泛的古代雕塑收藏的大英博物馆。在这些参观过程中，他发现了古埃及和非洲雕塑的力量和美。

target_sen.target_sen: Alongside the instruction he received at the Royal College, Moore visited many of the London museums, particularly the British Museum, which had a wide-ranging collection of ancient sculpture.
Alongside the instruction he received at the Royal College, Moore visited many of the London museums, particularly the British Museum, which had a wide-ranging collection of ancient sculpture.

questions.title: The Trocadero Museum's Mayan sculpture attracted a lot of public interest.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "The Trocadero Museum's Mayan sculpture attracted a lot of public interest" 在文章中没有提到。文章只提到 Henry Moore 对这尊玛雅雕塑印象深刻，但没有提到公众的兴趣。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到对应的表达，答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: When he visited the Trocadero Museum in Paris, he was impressed by a cast of a Mayan* sculpture of the rain spirit. It
central_sentences.translation: 当他参观巴黎的特罗卡德罗博物馆时，他被一个玛雅雨神雕塑的复制品所吸引。

target_sen.target_sen: When he visited the Trocadero Museum in Paris, he was impressed by a cast of a Mayan* sculpture of the rain spirit.
When he visited the Trocadero Museum in Paris, he was impressed by a cast of a Mayan* sculpture of the rain spirit.

questions.title: Moore thought the Mayan sculpture was similar in certain respects to other stone sculptures.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“similar”表示相似，而原文中明确提到摩尔认为玛雅雕塑具有“其他石雕所没有的力量和独创性”（no other stone sculpture possessed），这表明玛雅雕塑是独特的，与其他石雕不同。因此，题干所述与原文内容相反，所以答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Moore became fascinated with this stone sculpture, which he thought had a power and originality that no other stone sculpture possessed.
central_sentences.translation: 摩尔对这座石雕着迷，他认为这座石雕具有其他石雕所没有的力量和原创性。

target_sen.target_sen: When he visited the Trocadero Museum in Paris, he was impressed by a cast of a Mayan* sculpture of the rain spirit.
Moore became fascinated with this stone sculpture, which he thought had a power and originality that no other stone sculpture possessed.

questions.title: The artists who belonged to Unit One wanted to make modern art and architecture more popular.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息与原文中的信息完全匹配。题干中的“Unit One”在原文中直接提到，“wanted to make”对应“aim of the group”，“modern art and architecture more popular”对应“convince the English public of the merits of the emerging international movement in modern art and architecture”。因此，题干中的信息在原文中可以找到，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: The aim of the group was to convince the English public of the merits of the emerging international movement in modern art and architecture.
central_sentences.translation: 该组织的目标是让英国公众认识到新兴的国际现代艺术和建筑运动的价值。

target_sen.target_sen: In 1933, he became a member of a group of young artists called Unit One.

syn_ary.synonym: convince the English public of the merits of the emerging international movement in modern art and architecture

target_sen.target_sen: The aim of the group was to convince the English public of the merits of the emerging international movement in modern art and architecture.

locating_words.locating_word: make modern art and architecture more popular

groups.title: Moore's career as an artist
groups.desc:
Questions 8–13

Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer. 
Write your answers in boxes 8–13 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: 1930s • Moore's exhibition at the Leicester Galleries is criticised by the press • Moore is urged to offer his$562$and leave the Royal College 1940s • Moore turns to drawing because$563$for sculpting are not readily available • While visiting his hometown, Moore does some drawings of$564$ • Moore is employed to produce a sculpture of a$565$ $566$start to buy Moore's work • Moore's increased$567$makes it possible for him to do more ambitious sculptures 1950s • Moore's series of bronze figures marks a further change in his style

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "urged to offer" 对应文章中的 "calls for"，意思都是“被要求”。"leave the Royal College" 对应文章中的 "resignation from the Royal College"，意思都是“离开皇家学院”。因此，答案是 "resignation"。这个答案是正确的，因为它准确地反映了题干中所描述的情境，并且在文章中找到了对应的表达。

central_sentences.sentence: There were calls for his resignation from the Royal College, and the following year, when his contract expired, he left to start a sculpture department at the Chelsea School of Art in London.
central_sentences.translation: 有人呼吁他从皇家学院辞职，第二年，当他的合同到期时，他离开并在伦敦切尔西艺术学院开始创建雕塑系。

target_sen.target_sen: There were calls for his resignation from the Royal College, and the following year, when his contract expired, he left to start a sculpture department at the Chelsea School of Art in London.
There were calls for his resignation from the Royal College, and the following year, when his contract expired, he left to start a sculpture department at the Chelsea School of Art in London.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "materials"。题干中的 "turns to drawing" 对应原文中的 "focus on drawing"，"not readily available" 对应 "shortage"。原文句子 "A shortage of materials forced him to focus on drawing" 明确指出材料短缺是摩尔转向绘画的原因，因此答案是 "materials"。

central_sentences.sentence: A shortage of materials forced him to focus on drawing.
central_sentences.translation: 由于材料短缺，他被迫专注于绘画。

target_sen.target_sen: A shortage of materials forced him to focus on drawing.
A shortage of materials forced him to focus on drawing.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "hometown" 对应原文中的 "Castleford"，"drawings" 对应原文中的 "sketches"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确认答案所在的句子是 "In 1942, he returned to Castleford to make a series of sketches of the miners who worked there."。因此，Moore 在家乡画的是矿工（miners），所以答案是 "miners"。

central_sentences.sentence: In 1942, he returned to Castleford to make a series of sketches of the miners who worked there.
central_sentences.translation: 1942年，他回到卡斯尔福德，为在那里工作的矿工们画了一系列的素描。

target_sen.target_sen: In 1942, he returned to Castleford to make a series of sketches of the miners who worked there.
He did numerous small sketches of Londoners, later turning these ideas into large coloured drawings in his studio.
In 1942, he returned to Castleford to make a series of sketches of the miners who worked there.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“employed to produce”在原文中对应“offered a commission for”，“a sculpture of a【 】”对应“a sculpture depicting a family”。因此，答案是“family”，因为这是雕塑的具体内容。

central_sentences.sentence: In 1944, Harlow, a town near London, offered Moore a commission for a sculpture depicting a family.
central_sentences.translation: 1944年，伦敦附近的哈洛镇委托摩尔制作一座描绘家庭的雕塑。

target_sen.target_sen: In 1944, Harlow, a town near London, offered Moore a commission for a sculpture depicting a family.
He did dozens of studies in clay for the sculpture, and these were cast in bronze and issued in editions of seven to nine copies each.
The boost to his income enabled him to take on ambitious projects and start working on the scale he felt his sculpture demanded.

questions.analysis: 答案是“collectors”，因为题干中的“start to buy”在原文中对应的是“became available to”，而“Moore's work”在原文中也是直接提到的。原文句子“Moore's work became available to collectors all over the world”说明了摩尔的作品开始被收藏家购买。因此，正确答案是“collectors”。

central_sentences.sentence: In this way, Moore's work became available to collectors all over the world.
central_sentences.translation: 这样，摩尔的作品开始在全世界的收藏家之间流通。

target_sen.target_sen: In this way, Moore's work became available to collectors all over the world.
In this way, Moore's work became available to collectors all over the world.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“increased”在原文中对应的是“boost”，“ambitious sculptures”在原文中对应的是“ambitious projects”。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以定位到答案句。答案句中明确提到“boost to his income”使他能够进行更有野心的项目，因此答案是“income”。

central_sentences.sentence: The boost to his income enabled him to take on ambitious projects and start working on the scale he felt his sculpture demanded.
central_sentences.translation: 他收入的增加使他能够承担雄心勃勃的项目，并开始以他认为雕塑所需要的规模进行创作。

target_sen.target_sen: The boost to his income enabled him to take on ambitious projects and start working on the scale he felt his sculpture demanded.
The boost to his income enabled him to take on ambitious projects and start working on the scale he felt his sculpture demanded.

content.title: The Desolenator: producing clean water
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了William Janssen在20世纪90年代游历泰国时对许多家庭使用的基本屋顶太阳能加热系统印象深刻，他将这个想法发展成了一种利用太阳能净化水的便携设备。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了Desolenator作为一种移动海水淡化装置，可以从不同的地方获取水源，如海水、河流、钻井和雨水，并将其净化供人类饮用。它在天然地下水储备被污染或海水是唯一的水源的地区尤其有价值。Janssen在移居阿拉伯联合酋长国并目睹大规模水处理后，意识到无论是发展中国家还是发达国家都需要一种可持续的水处理方式。他说：“我面对的是海湾国家由于所有淡化处理而产生的巨大碳足迹。”', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了Desolenator能够利用太阳能产生15升饮用水，足够维持一个家庭的烹饪和饮用需求，并且不需要外部电源供应，非常便于携带和使用。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了全球至少三分之二的人口每年至少有一个月面临严重的水资源匮乏问题，到2030年，全球一半的人口将面临水资源紧缺的压力，而许多国家由于缺乏资金无法建设和运营昂贵的海水淡化厂，导致水资源问题日益严重。', 'E': '该设备旨在满足广泛的用户需求，包括发展中国家没有持续供水的居民以及美国农村地区的离网居民。预计Desolenator的首批商业版本将在明年初在印度投入使用，经过现场测试。在发展中国家，这种自给自足的设备的市场有两个方面：一是那些无法一次性支付设备费用的人通过小额信贷支付，二是中等收入家庭可以租赁自己的设备。在印度，人们不会一次性购买冰箱，而是分期付款，他们会将Desolenator安装在屋顶并与市政供水系统连接，每天都能得到可靠的饮用水。在发达国家，该设备主要面向无法获得自来水的小众市场，例如露营、船上或军事用途。', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了该产品的价格将根据购买地点的不同而有所变化，发展中国家的价格将取决于援助组织能够谈判的交易，而发达国家的价格可能为每台1000美元（685英镑），该公司意识到该产品主要在发展中国家和人道主义领域找到应用，但也要考虑到公司的盈利底线。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了一家总部位于伦敦帝国理工学院的公司计划在两年内在人道主义领域销售每月1000台产品，并计划在澳大利亚、智利北部、秘鲁、德克萨斯州和加利福尼亚等地销售。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Travelling around Thailand in the 1990s, William Janssen was impressed with the basic rooftop solar heating systems that were on many homes, where energy from the sun was absorbed by a plate and then used to heat water for domestic use. Two decades later Janssen developed that basic idea he saw in Southeast Asia into a portable device that uses the power from the sun to purify water.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text:
The Desolenator operates as a mobile desalination unit that can take water from different places, such as the sea, rivers, boreholes and rain, and purify it for human consumption. It is particularly valuable in regions where natural groundwater reserves have been polluted, or where seawater is the only water source available.
Janssen saw that there was a need for a sustainable way to clean water in both the developing and the developed countries when he moved to the United Arab Emirates and saw large-scale water processing. 'I was confronted with the enormous carbon footprint that the Gulf nations have because of all of the desalination that they do,' he says.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
The Desolenator can produce 15 litres of drinking water per day, enough to sustain a family for cooking and drinking. Its main selling point is that unlike standard desalination techniques, it doesn't require a generated power supply: just sunlight. It measures 120 cm by 90 cm, and is easy to transport, thanks to its two wheels. Water enters through a pipe, and flows as a thin film between a sheet of double glazing and the surface of a solar panel, where it is heated by the sun. The warm water flows into a small boiler (heated by a solar-powered battery) where it is converted to steam.
When the steam cools, it becomes distilled water. The device has a very simple filter to trap particles, and this can easily be shaken to remove them. There are two tubes for liquid coming out: one for the waste – salt from seawater, fluoride, etc. – and another for the distilled water. The performance of the unit is shown on an LCD screen and transmitted to the company which provides servicing when necessary.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: A recent analysis found that at least two-thirds of the world's population lives with severe water scarcity for at least a month every year. Janssen says that by 2030 half of the world's population will be living with water stress – where the demand exceeds the supply over a certain period of time. 'It is really important that a sustainable solution is brought to the market that is able to help these people,' he says. Many countries 'don't have the money for desalination plants, which are very expensive to build. They don't have the money to operate them, they are very maintenance intensive, and they don't have the money to buy the diesel to run the desalination plants, so it is a really bad situation.'

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The device is aimed at a wide variety of users – from homeowners in the developing world who do not have a constant supply of water to people living off the grid in rural parts of the US. The first commercial versions of the Desolenator are expected to be in operation in India early next year, after field tests are carried out. The market for the self-sufficient devices in developing countries is twofold – those who cannot afford the money for the device outright and pay through microfinance, and middle-income homes that can lease their own equipment. 'People in India don't pay for a fridge outright; they pay for it over six months. They would put the Desolenator on their roof and hook it up to their municipal supply and they would get very reliable drinking water on a daily basis,' Janssen says. In the developed world, it is aimed at niche markets where tap water is unavailable – for camping, on boats, or for the military, for instance.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Prices will vary according to where it is bought. In the developing world, the price will depend on what deal aid organisations can negotiate. In developed countries, it is likely to come in at $1,000 (£685) a unit, said Janssen. 'We are a venture with a social mission. We are aware that the product we have envisioned is mainly finding application in the developing world and humanitarian sector and that this is the way we will proceed. We do realise, though, that to be a viable company there is a bottom line to keep in mind,' he says.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The company itself is based at Imperial College London, although Janssen, its chief executive, still lives in the UAE. It has raised £340,000 in funding so far. Within two years, he says, the company aims to be selling 1,000 units a month, mainly in the humanitarian field. They are expected to be sold in areas such as Australia, northern Chile, Peru, Texas and California.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 14–20
Reading Passage 2 has seven sections, A–G.
Choose the correct heading for each section from the list of headings below. 
Write the correct number, i–x, in boxes 14–20 on your answer sheet.

list.text: From initial inspiration to new product
The range of potential customers for the device
What makes the device different from alternatives
Cleaning water from a range of sources
A warm welcome for the device
The number of people affected by water shortages

questions.analysis: 这段文字描述了威廉·詹森从在泰国旅行时受到的启发，到二十年后开发出一种新产品的过程。小标题iii: "From initial inspiration to new product"正好概括了这一过程，因此是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Travelling around Thailand in the 1990s, William Janssen was impressed with the basic rooftop solar heating systems that were on many homes, where energy from the sun was absorbed by a plate and then used to heat water for domestic use. Two decades later Janssen developed that basic idea he saw in Southeast Asia into a portable device that uses the power from the sun to purify water.
central_sentences.translation: 20世纪90年代，William Janssen在泰国旅行时对许多家庭屋顶上的基本太阳能加热系统印象深刻，这种系统通过吸收太阳能来加热生活用水。二十年后，Janssen将他在东南亚看到的这个基本想法发展成了一种便携式设备，利用太阳能来净化水。

syn_ary.synonym: Travelling around Thailand in the 1990s, William Janssen was impressed with the basic rooftop solar heating systems

target_sen.target_sen: Travelling around Thailand in the 1990s, William Janssen was impressed with the basic rooftop solar heating systems that were on many homes, where energy from the sun was absorbed by a plate and then used to heat water for domestic use.

syn_ary.synonym: Two decades later Janssen developed that basic idea he saw in Southeast Asia into a portable device that uses the power from the sun to purify water

target_sen.target_sen: Two decades later Janssen developed that basic idea he saw in Southeast Asia into a portable device that uses the power from the sun to purify water.

questions.analysis: Section B的内容描述了Desolenator如何从多种水源中获取水并进行净化，这与标题vi“Cleaning water from a range of sources”直接对应。段落中的“different places, such as the sea, rivers, boreholes and rain”与“range of sources”对应，而“purify it for human consumption”与“Cleaning water”对应。因此，vi是最合适的标题。

central_sentences.sentence: The Desolenator operates as a mobile desalination unit that can take water from different places, such as the sea, rivers, boreholes and rain, and purify it for human consumption.
central_sentences.translation: Desolenator作为一个移动脱盐装置运行，可以从海洋、河流、钻孔和雨水等不同地方获取水，并将其净化以供人类使用。

target_sen.target_sen: The Desolenator operates as a mobile desalination unit that can take water from different places, such as the sea, rivers, boreholes and rain, and purify it for human consumption.

syn_ary.synonym: different places, such as the sea, rivers, boreholes and rain

target_sen.target_sen: The Desolenator operates as a mobile desalination unit that can take water from different places, such as the sea, rivers, boreholes and rain, and purify it for human consumption.

questions.analysis: Section C详细描述了Desolenator设备的工作原理和特点，特别强调了它与传统海水淡化技术的不同之处。关键句子如“unlike standard desalination techniques, it doesn't require a generated power supply: just sunlight”明确指出了设备的独特之处，这正是标题v: What makes the device different from alternatives所描述的内容。因此，Section C对应的小标题是v。

central_sentences.sentence: Its main selling point is that unlike standard desalination techniques, it doesn't require a generated power supply: just sunlight.
central_sentences.translation: 它的主要卖点是，与标准的海水淡化技术不同，它不需要生成的电力供应：只需要阳光。

target_sen.target_sen: Its main selling point is that unlike standard desalination techniques, it doesn't require a generated power supply: just sunlight.
Its main selling point is that unlike standard desalination techniques, it doesn't require a generated power supply: just sunlight.

questions.analysis: Section D 主要讨论了全球水资源短缺的问题，提供了具体的统计数据和未来的预测。这些内容与小标题 x: The number of people affected by water shortages 完全对应，因为它们都在讨论受水资源短缺影响的人数。

central_sentences.sentence: A recent analysis found that at least two-thirds of the world's population lives with severe water scarcity for at least a month every year.
central_sentences.translation: 最近的一项分析发现，全球至少三分之二的人口每年至少有一个月面临严重的缺水问题。

central_sentences.sentence: Janssen says that by 2030 half of the world's population will be living with water stress – where the demand exceeds the supply over a certain period of time.
central_sentences.translation: 扬森说，到2030年，全球一半的人口将面临水资源压力——即在一定时期内需求超过供给。

questions.analysis: Section E的内容主要讨论了Desolenator设备的潜在用户群体，包括发展中国家和发达国家的不同用户群体。这与标题“iv: The range of potential customers for the device”直接对应。文章中提到的“wide variety of users”和“market”是“potential customers”的同义替换词，明确指出了设备的目标用户范围。

central_sentences.sentence: The device is aimed at a wide variety of users – from homeowners in the developing world who do not have a constant supply of water to people living off the grid in rural parts of the US.
central_sentences.translation: 该设备针对的是各种各样的用户——从发展中国家那些没有稳定供水的房主，到美国农村地区脱离电网生活的人。

target_sen.target_sen: The device is aimed at a wide variety of users – from homeowners in the developing world who do not have a constant supply of water to people living off the grid in rural parts of the US.

The market for the self-sufficient devices in developing countries is twofold – those who cannot afford the money for the device outright and pay through microfinance, and middle- income homes that can lease their own equipment.
In the developed world, it is aimed at niche markets where tap water is unavailable – for camping, on boats, or for the military, for instance.
The first commercial versions of the Desolenator are expected to be in operation in India early next year, after field tests are carried out.
They would put the Desolenator on their roof and hook it up to their municipal supply and they would get very reliable drinking water on a daily basis,' Janssen says.

The market for the self-sufficient devices in developing countries is twofold – those who cannot afford the money for the device outright and pay through microfinance, and middle- income homes that can lease their own equipment.

questions.analysis: Section F的主要内容是公司如何在追求社会使命和人道主义目标的同时，保持商业可行性。这与小标题viii: Profit not the primary goal完全一致。通过找到段落中的同义表达，理解段落的主要内容，并排除不相关的小标题，学生可以正确选择viii作为Section F的小标题。

central_sentences.sentence: We are a venture with a social mission. We are aware that the product we have envisioned is mainly finding application in the developing world and humanitarian sector and that this is the way we will proceed. We do realise, though, that to be a viable company there is a bottom line to keep in mind
central_sentences.translation: 我们是一家有社会使命的企业。我们意识到我们设想的产品主要在发展中国家和人道主义领域应用，这也是我们将继续推进的方向。不过，我们也意识到，要成为一家可行的公司，我们还是需要考虑盈利底线的。

target_sen.target_sen: We do realise, though, that to be a viable company there is a bottom line to keep in mind,' he says.
'We are a venture with a social mission.

questions.analysis: Section G的内容主要描述了公司筹集资金的情况以及未来的生产计划。关键句子是“它已经筹集了34万英镑的资金”和“公司计划每月销售1000台设备”，这与小标题i: Getting the finance for production（为生产筹集资金）完全对应。因此，Section G的最佳小标题是i。

central_sentences.sentence: It has raised £340,000 in funding so far.
central_sentences.translation: 到目前为止，该公司已经筹集了34万英镑的资金。

target_sen.target_sen: It has raised £340,000 in funding so far.
Within two years, he says, the company aims to be selling 1,000 units a month, mainly in the humanitarian field.

groups.title: How the Desolenator works
groups.desc:
Questions 21–26

Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer. 
Write your answers in boxes 21–26 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: The energy required to operate the Desolenator comes from sunlight. The device can be used in different locations, as it has$575$. Water is fed into a pipe, and a$576$of water flows over a solar panel. The water then enters a boiler, where it turns into steam. Any particles in the water are caught in a $577$. The purified water comes out through one tube, and all types of$578$come out through another. A screen displays the$579$of the device, and transmits the information to the company so that they know when the Desolenator requires$580$.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "wheels"。题干中的 "different locations" 对应原文中的 "easy to transport"，而 "has" 对应 "thanks to its"。这表明设备易于在不同地点使用的原因是它有轮子。因此，正确答案是 "wheels"。

central_sentences.sentence: It measures 120 cm by 90 cm, and is easy to transport, thanks to its two wheels.
central_sentences.translation: 它的尺寸为120厘米×90厘米，由于有两个轮子，所以很容易运输。

target_sen.target_sen: It measures 120 cm by 90 cm, and is easy to transport, thanks to its two wheels.
It measures 120 cm by 90 cm, and is easy to transport, thanks to its two wheels.

questions.analysis: 答案是“film”，因为题干中的“flows over a solar panel”在原文中对应的是“flows as a thin film between... and the surface of a solar panel”。这里的“thin film”描述了水流的状态，符合题干的要求。通过匹配题干中的关键词“pipe”、“flows over”和“solar panel”与原文中的对应词，可以确定答案所在的句子，并找到正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Water enters through a pipe, and flows as a thin film between a sheet of double glazing and the surface of a solar panel, where it is heated by the sun.
central_sentences.translation: 水通过一根管道进入，然后作为一层薄膜流过双层玻璃和太阳能板表面之间，在那里被太阳加热。

target_sen.target_sen: Water enters through a pipe, and flows as a thin film between a sheet of double glazing and the surface of a solar panel, where it is heated by the sun.
Water enters through a pipe, and flows as a thin film between a sheet of double glazing and the surface of a solar panel, where it is heated by the sun.
Water enters through a pipe, and flows as a thin film between a sheet of double glazing and the surface of a solar panel, where it is heated by the sun.

questions.analysis: 答案是“filter”，因为题干中的“particles”在原文中直接对应“particles”，而“caught”在原文中对应“trap”。通过这些同义词的对比，可以确认答案所在的句子，并找到“filter”这个词。题干问的是捕获颗粒的东西，而“filter”正是捕获颗粒的装置。

central_sentences.sentence: The device has a very simple filter to trap particles, and this can easily be shaken to remove them.
central_sentences.translation: 该设备有一个非常简单的过滤器来捕获颗粒，可以轻易地摇动来去除这些颗粒。

target_sen.target_sen: The device has a very simple filter to trap particles, and this can easily be shaken to remove them.
The device has a very simple filter to trap particles, and this can easily be shaken to remove them.

questions.analysis: 答案是“waste”，因为题干中的“purified water”对应文章中的“distilled water”，“tube”对应“tubes”，“all types of”对应“one for the waste – salt from seawater, fluoride, etc.”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案句中的“waste”是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: There are two tubes for liquid coming out: one for the waste – salt from seawater, fluoride, etc. – and another for the distilled water.
central_sentences.translation: 有两根管子用于液体输出：一根用于废物——海水中的盐、氟化物等，另一根用于蒸馏水。

target_sen.target_sen: When the steam cools, it becomes distilled water.
– and another for the distilled water.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "performance"。题干中的 "A screen displays the【 】" 对应原文中的 "is shown on an LCD screen"，而 "transmits the information to the company" 对应 "transmitted to the company"。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案所在的句子是 "The performance of the unit is shown on an LCD screen and transmitted to the company which provides servicing when necessary."。因此，正确答案是 "performance"，因为它是文章中与题干中 "A screen displays the【 】" 最直接对应的词汇。

central_sentences.sentence: The performance of the unit is shown on an LCD screen and transmitted to the company which provides servicing when necessary.

content.title: Why fairy tales are really scary tales

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: People of every culture tell each other fairy tales, but the same story often takes a variety of forms in different parts of the world. In the story of Little Red Riding Hood that European children are familiar with, a young girl on the way to see her grandmother meets a wolf and tells him where she is going. The wolf runs on ahead and disposes of the grandmother, then gets into bed dressed in the grandmother's clothes to wait for Little Red Riding Hood. You may think you know the story – but which version? In some versions, the wolf swallows up the grandmother, while in others it locks her in a cupboard. In some stories Red Riding Hood gets the better of the wolf on her own, while in others a hunter or a woodcutter hears her cries and comes to her rescue.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The universal appeal of these tales is frequently attributed to the idea that they contain cautionary messages: in the case of Little Red Riding Hood, to listen to your mother, and avoid talking to strangers. 'It might be what we find interesting about this story is that it's got this survival-relevant information in it,' says anthropologist Jamie Tehrani at Durham University in the UK. But his research suggests otherwise. 'We have this huge gap in our knowledge about the history and prehistory of storytelling, despite the fact that we know this genre is an incredibly ancient one,' he says. That hasn't stopped anthropologists, folklorists* and other academics devising theories to explain the importance of fairy tales in human society. Now Tehrani has found a way to test these ideas, borrowing a technique from evolutionary biologists.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: To work out the evolutionary history, development and relationships among groups of organisms, biologists compare the characteristics of living species in a process called 'phylogenetic analysis'. Tehrani has used the same approach to compare related versions of fairy tales to discover how they have evolved and which elements have survived longest.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Tehrani's analysis focused on Little Red Riding Hood in its many forms, which include another Western fairy tale known as The Wolf and the Kids. Checking for variants of these two tales and similar stories from Africa, East Asia and other regions, he ended up with 58 stories recorded from oral traditions. Once his phylogenetic analysis had established that they were indeed related, he used the same methods to explore how they have developed and altered over time.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: First he tested some assumptions about which aspects of the story alter least as it evolves, indicating their importance. Folklorists believe that what happens in a story is more central to the story than the characters in it – that visiting a relative, only to be met by a scary animal in disguise, is more fundamental than whether the visitor is a little girl or three siblings, or the animal is a tiger instead of a wolf.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: However, Tehrani found no significant difference in the rate of evolution of incidents compared with that of characters. 'Certain episodes are very stable because they are crucial to the story, but there are lots of other details that can evolve quite freely,' he says. Neither did his analysis support the theory that the central section of a story is the most conserved part. He found no significant difference in the flexibility of events there compared with the beginning or the end.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: But the really big surprise came when he looked at the cautionary elements of the story. 'Studies on hunter-gatherer folk tales suggest that these narratives include really important information about the environment and the possible dangers that may be faced there – stuff that's relevant to survival,' he says. Yet in his analysis such elements were just as flexible as seemingly trivial details. What, then, is important enough to be reproduced from generation to generation?

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The answer, it would appear, is fear – blood-thirsty and gruesome aspects of the story, such as the eating of the grandmother by the wolf, turned out to be the best preserved of all. Why are these details retained by generations of storytellers, when other features are not? Tehrani has an idea: 'In an oral context, a story won't survive because of one great teller. It also needs to be interesting when it's told by someone who's not necessarily a great storyteller.' Maybe being swallowed whole by a wolf, then cut out of its stomach alive is so gripping that it helps the story remain popular, no matter how badly it's told.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Jack Zipes at the University of Minnesota, Minneapolis, is unconvinced by Tehrani's views on fairy tales. 'Even if they're gruesome, they won't stick unless they matter,' he says. He believes the perennial theme of women as victims in stories like Little Red Riding Hood explains why they continue to feel relevant. But Tehrani points out that although this is often the case in Western versions, it is not always true elsewhere. In Chinese and Japanese versions, often known as The Tiger Grandmother, the villain is a woman, and in both Iran and Nigeria, the victim is a boy.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Mathias Clasen at Aarhus University in Denmark isn't surprised by Tehrani's findings. 'Habits and morals change, but the things that scare us, and the fact that we seek out entertainment that's designed to scare us – those are constant,' he says. Clasen believes that scary stories teach us what it feels like to be afraid without having to experience real danger, and so build up resistance to negative emotions.

groups.desc:
Questions 27–31

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A–F, below. 
Write the correct letter, A–F, in boxes 27–31 on your answer sheet.

list.text: may be provided through methods used in biological research.
are the reason for their survival.
contain animals which transform to become humans.
were originally spoken rather than written.
have been developed without factual basis.

questions.title: In fairy tales, details of the plot
questions.analysis: 文章中提到“People of every culture tell each other fairy tales but the same story often takes a variety of forms in different parts of the world.”，这句话说明了童话故事在全球范围内有多种形式，这与题干中的“details of the plot”直接对应。因此，正确答案是C: show considerable global variation。

central_sentences.sentence: People of every culture tell each other fairy tales but the same story often takes a variety of forms in different parts of the world. In
central_sentences.translation: 每个文化的人都互相讲述童话故事，但同一个故事在世界不同地方常常有多种形式。

target_sen.target_sen: People of every culture tell each other fairy tales but the same story often takes a variety of forms in different parts of the world.
People of every culture tell each other fairy tales but the same story often takes a variety of forms in different parts of the world.

questions.title: Tehrani rejects the idea that the useful lessons for life in fairy tales
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Tehrani rejects the idea that the useful lessons for life in fairy tales”意思是Tehrani反对童话故事中有用的生活教训是其存续原因的观点。文章中提到，Tehrani认为这些故事的吸引力通常被归因于它们包含警示信息（useful lessons for life），但他的研究表明并非如此（his research suggests otherwise）。因此，正确答案是B: are the reason for their survival。

central_sentences.sentence: The universal appeal of these tales is frequently attributed to the idea that they contain cautionary messages: in the case of Little Red Riding Hood, to listen to your mother, and avoid talking to strangers. 'It might be what we find interesting about this story is that it's got this survival- relevant information in it,' says anthropologist Jamie Tehrani at Durham University in the UK. But his research suggests otherwise. 'We
central_sentences.translation: 这些故事的普遍吸引力通常被认为源于它们包含警示信息：例如，在小红帽的故事中，要听妈妈的话，不要和陌生人说话。英国杜伦大学的人类学家Jamie Tehrani说："我们可能觉得这个故事有趣是因为它包含了与生存相关的信息。"但他的研究表明情况并非如此。

target_sen.target_sen: But his research suggests otherwise.

The universal appeal of these tales is frequently attributed to the idea that they contain cautionary messages: in the case of Little Red Riding Hood, to listen to your mother, and avoid talking to strangers.
'It might be what we find interesting about this story is that it's got this survival- relevant information in it,' says anthropologist Jamie Tehrani at Durham University in the UK.

questions.title: Various theories about the social significance of fairy tales
questions.analysis: 文章提到，尽管我们对童话故事的历史和史前历史了解甚少，但学者们还是制定了许多理论来解释童话故事在社会中的重要性。这表明这些理论缺乏事实基础。因此，正确答案是F: have been developed without factual basis。

central_sentences.sentence: That hasn't stopped anthropologists, folklorists* and other academics devising theories to explain the importance of fairy tales in human society.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管我们对故事讲述的历史和史前历史了解甚少，但这并未阻止人类学家、民俗学家和其他学者制定理论来解释童话故事在人类社会中的重要性。

target_sen.target_sen: That hasn't stopped anthropologists, folklorists* and other academics devising theories to explain the importance of fairy tales in human society.
That hasn't stopped anthropologists, folklorists* and other academics devising theories to explain the importance of fairy tales in human society.
That hasn't stopped anthropologists, folklorists* and other academics devising theories to explain the importance of fairy tales in human society.

questions.title: Insights into the development of fairy tales
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Insights into the development of fairy tales”意思是“对童话故事发展的见解”。在文章中，Tehrani 使用了进化生物学家的技术来研究童话故事的发展，这与题干中的意思相符。因此，正确答案是 A: may be provided through methods used in biological research。

central_sentences.sentence: Now Tehrani has found a way to test these ideas, borrowing a technique from evolutionary biologists.
central_sentences.translation: 现在，Tehrani找到了一种测试这些想法的方法，借鉴了进化生物学家的技术。

target_sen.target_sen: That hasn't stopped anthropologists, folklorists* and other academics devising theories to explain the importance of fairy tales in human society.

questions.title: All the fairy tales analysed by Tehrani
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 E，因为文章明确提到 Tehrani 分析的童话故事是“recorded from oral traditions”，即这些故事最初是口头流传的，而不是书面的。这与选项 E 的“were originally spoken rather than written”完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Tehrani's analysis focused on Little Red Riding Hood in its many forms, which include another Western fairy tale known as The Wolf and the Kids. Checking for variants of these two tales and similar stories from Africa, East Asia and other regions, he ended up with 58 stories recorded from oral traditions.
central_sentences.translation: Tehrani的分析主要集中在《小红帽》的多种形式上，其中包括另一个西方童话故事《狼和小孩》。他检查了这两个故事的变体以及来自非洲、东亚和其他地区的类似故事，最终得到了58个从口头传统中记录下来的故事。

target_sen.target_sen: Checking for variants of these two tales and similar stories from Africa, East Asia and other regions, he ended up with 58 stories recorded from oral traditions.
Tehrani's analysis focused on Little Red Riding Hood in its many forms, which include another Western fairy tale known as The Wolf and the Kids.

groups.title: Phylogenetic analysis of Little Red Riding Hood
groups.desc:
Questions 32–36

Complete the summary using the list of words, A–I, below.
Write the correct letter, A–I, in boxes 32–36 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: Tehrani used techniques from evolutionary biology to find out if $586$existed among 58 stories from around the world. He also wanted to know which aspects of the stories had fewest $587$, as he believed these aspects would be the most important ones. Contrary to other beliefs, he found that some$588$that were included in a story tended to change over time, and that the middle of a story seemed no more important than the other parts. He was also surprised that parts of a story which seemed to provide some sort of$589$were unimportant. The aspect that he found most important in a story's survival was $590$.

questions.analysis: 题干中提到的 "find out if ... existed among 58 stories" 其实是在问这些故事之间是否存在联系。结合文章内容 "established that they were indeed related" 的信息，可以确定正确答案是 "links"。

central_sentences.sentence: Once his phylogenetic analysis had established that they were indeed related, he used the same methods to explore how they have developed and altered over time.
central_sentences.translation: 一旦他的系统发生分析确定了这些故事确实有关联，他就使用同样的方法来探究它们是如何随时间发展和变化的。

target_sen.target_sen: Once his phylogenetic analysis had established that they were indeed related, he used the same methods to explore how they have developed and altered over time.
Checking for variants of these two tales and similar stories from Africa, East Asia and other regions, he ended up with 58 stories recorded from oral traditions.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "fewest" 对应原文中的 "least"，"aspects of the stories" 对应 "aspects of the story"，"important" 对应 "indicating their importance"。通过这些同义替换词，可以确定答案句为 "First he tested some assumptions about which aspects of the story alter least as it evolves, indicating their importance."。因此，最少变化的方面是最重要的，答案为 "variations"。

central_sentences.sentence: First he tested some assumptions about which aspects of the story alter least as it evolves, indicating their importance.
central_sentences.translation: 首先，他测试了一些假设，即哪些故事方面在演变过程中变化最少，这表明了它们的重要性。

target_sen.target_sen: First he tested some assumptions about which aspects of the story alter least as it evolves, indicating their importance.
First he tested some assumptions about which aspects of the story alter least as it evolves, indicating their importance.

questions.analysis: 题干中提到的“some【 】that were included in a story tended to change over time”对应文章中的“lots of other details that can evolve quite freely”，而“the middle of a story seemed no more important than the other parts”对应文章中的“no significant difference in the flexibility of events there compared with the beginning or the end”。由此可以判断，符合描述的答案应该是与“details that can evolve”相关的词，即“events”。

central_sentences.sentence: However, Tehrani found no significant difference in the rate of evolution of incidents compared with that of characters. 'Certain episodes are very stable because they are crucial to the story, but there are lots of other details that can evolve quite freely,' he says. Neither did his analysis support the theory that the central section of a story is the most conserved part. He found no significant difference in the flexibility of events there compared with the beginning or the end.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，Tehrani发现事件的演变速度与角色的演变速度没有显著差异。他说："某些情节因为对故事至关重要而保持稳定，但是有很多其他细节可以自由演变。"他的分析也不支持故事的中心部分是最保守的这一理论。他发现中心部分的事件灵活性与开头或结尾相比没有显著差异。

target_sen.target_sen: 'Certain episodes are very stable because they are crucial to the story, but there are lots of other details that can evolve quite freely,' he says.
Neither did his analysis support the theory that the central section of a story is the most conserved part.

syn_ary.synonym: no significant difference in the flexibility of events there compared with the beginning or the end

target_sen.target_sen: He found no significant difference in the flexibility of events there compared with the beginning or the end.

locating_words.locating_word: no more important than the other parts

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "surprised" 对应原文中的 "surprise"，"provide some sort of" 对应 "cautionary elements"，"unimportant" 对应 "flexible as seemingly trivial details"。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案是 "warning"。因为文章中提到的 "cautionary elements" 就是警示元素，而这些元素在分析中被认为和琐碎的细节一样灵活，即不重要。

central_sentences.sentence: But the really big surprise came when he looked at the cautionary elements of the story. 'Studies on hunter-gatherer folk tales suggest that these narratives include really important information about the environment and the possible dangers that may be faced there – stuff that's relevant to survival,' he says. Yet in his analysis such elements were just as flexible as seemingly trivial details.
central_sentences.translation: 但当他查看故事中的警示元素时，出现了一个真正的大惊喜。他说："关于狩猎采集者民间故事的研究表明，这些叙述包含了关于环境和可能面临的危险的重要信息——与生存相关的内容。"然而，在他的分析中，这些元素与看似琐碎的细节一样灵活。

target_sen.target_sen: But the really big surprise came when he looked at the cautionary elements of the story.
Yet in his analysis such elements were just as flexible as seemingly trivial details.
Yet in his analysis such elements were just as flexible as seemingly trivial details.

questions.analysis: 文章中明确指出，故事中血腥和可怕的部分（horror）是保存得最好的，这意味着这些恐怖的元素是故事存续的最重要因素。因此，正确答案是 "horror"。

central_sentences.sentence: The answer, it would appear, is fear – blood-thirsty and gruesome aspects of the story, such as the eating of the grandmother by the wolf, turned out to be the best preserved of all.
central_sentences.translation: 答案似乎是恐惧——故事中血腥和可怕的方面，比如狼吃掉祖母，结果是所有内容中保存得最好的。

target_sen.target_sen: The answer, it would appear, is fear – blood-thirsty and gruesome aspects of the story, such as the eating of the grandmother by the wolf, turned out to be the best preserved of all.
The answer, it would appear, is fear – blood-thirsty and gruesome aspects of the story, such as the eating of the grandmother by the wolf, turned out to be the best preserved of all.

groups.desc:
Questions 37–40

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: What method did Jamie Tehrani use to test his ideas about fairy tales?

options.text: He compared oral and written forms of the same stories.
He looked at many different forms of the same basic story.
He looked at unrelated stories from many different countries.
He contrasted the development of fairy tales with that of living creatures.

questions.analysis: Jamie Tehrani使用了一种方法来比较不同版本的童话故事，以发现它们是如何演变的以及哪些元素存活时间最长。这个方法与选项B "He looked at many different forms of the same basic story"（他研究了同一基本故事的许多不同形式）最为匹配。

central_sentences.sentence: Tehrani has used the same approach to compare related versions of fairy tales to discover how they have evolved and which elements have survived longest.
central_sentences.translation: Tehrani使用了相同的方法来比较童话故事的相关版本，以发现它们是如何演变的以及哪些元素存活时间最长。

target_sen.target_sen: Tehrani has used the same approach to compare related versions of fairy tales to discover how they have evolved and which elements have survived longest.
To work out the evolutionary history, development and relationships among groups of organisms, biologists compare the characteristics of living species in a process called 'phylogenetic analysis'.
Tehrani has used the same approach to compare related versions of fairy tales to discover how they have evolved and which elements have survived longest.

questions.title: When discussing Tehrani's views, Jack Zipes suggests that

options.text: Tehrani ignores key changes in the role of women.
stories which are too horrific are not always taken seriously.
Tehrani overemphasises the importance of violence in stories.
features of stories only survive if they have a deeper significance.

questions.analysis: Jack Zipes 认为故事中的元素只有在具有更深层次意义时才会被保留下来。这与选项 D "features of stories only survive if they have a deeper significance" 完全一致。因此，选项 D 是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: 'Even if they're gruesome, they won't stick unless they matter,' he says.
central_sentences.translation: 他说："即使它们很可怕，如果没有意义，它们也不会留存下来。"

syn_ary.synonym: Jack Zipes at the University of Minnesota, Minneapolis

target_sen.target_sen: Jack Zipes at the University of Minnesota, Minneapolis, is unconvinced by Tehrani's views on fairy tales.
Jack Zipes at the University of Minnesota, Minneapolis, is unconvinced by Tehrani's views on fairy tales.

questions.title: Why does Tehrani refer to Chinese and Japanese fairy tales?

options.text: to indicate that Jack Zipes' theory is incorrect
to suggest that crime is a global problem
to imply that all fairy tales have a similar meaning
to add more evidence for Jack Zipes' ideas

questions.analysis: Tehrani提到中国和日本的童话故事是为了反驳Jack Zipes的观点。Zipes认为妇女作为受害者是这些故事持续存在的原因，但Tehrani指出，在中国和日本的版本中，反派是女性，这与Zipes的观点相矛盾。因此，Tehrani引用这些例子是为了表明Zipes的理论是不正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: But Tehrani points out that although this is often the case in Western versions, it is not always true elsewhere. In Chinese and Japanese versions, often known as The Tiger Grandmother, the villain is a woman, and in both Iran and Nigeria, the victim is a boy.
central_sentences.translation: 但特拉尼指出，虽然这在西方版本中通常是这样，但在其他地方并非总是如此。在中国和日本的版本中，通常被称为"虎姥姥"，反派是一个女人，而在伊朗和尼日利亚，受害者是一个男孩。

target_sen.target_sen: In Chinese and Japanese versions, often known as The Tiger Grandmother, the villain is a woman, and in both Iran and Nigeria, the victim is a boy.

questions.title: What does Mathias Clasen believe about fairy tales?

options.text: They are a safe way of learning to deal with fear.
They are a type of entertainment that some people avoid.
They reflect the changing values of our society.
They reduce our ability to deal with real-world problems.

questions.analysis: Mathias Clasen 认为童话故事（尤其是恐怖故事）是一种安全的方式，让我们在不必经历真正危险的情况下学会应对恐惧，从而建立对负面情绪的抵抗力。因此，选项 A "They are a safe way of learning to deal with fear." 是正确的，因为它准确地反映了 Clasen 的观点。

central_sentences.sentence: Clasen believes that scary stories teach us what it feels like to be afraid without having to experience real danger, and so build up resistance to negative emotions.
central_sentences.translation: Clasen认为，恐怖故事教会我们在不经历真实危险的情况下体验恐惧的感觉，从而建立起对负面情绪的抵抗力。

target_sen.target_sen: Mathias Clasen at Aarhus University in Denmark isn't surprised by Tehrani's findings.
Clasen believes that scary stories teach us what it feels like to be afraid without having to experience real danger, and so build up resistance to negative emotions.
